blockingIssues.description: VAL-CROSS-004 remains unproven because tests simulate cancellation via non-TTY guard failure and do not exercise an actual interactive cancellation flow followed by rerun evidence.
VAL-CROSS-005 remains unproven because parity checks compare only non-interactive flag variants and do not establish equivalence against interactive flow outputs for equal intent.

appliedUpdates.description: Added environment note that `oxfmt` is not always on PATH and checks should use `bun run format:check` or `npx oxfmt` for ad-hoc formatting validation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how to validate interactive assertions in non-interactive worker sessions: define accepted deterministic substitutes for VAL-CROSS-004/005 evidence and explicitly state when manual interactive checks are required vs optional for test-only or strict-typing fixes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three reviews in this milestone flagged the same mismatch: `.factory/skills/cli-refactor-worker/SKILL.md` currently mandates manual interactive verification while mission-level guidance allows deterministic alternatives, leading to inconsistent evidence interpretation and one blocking review failure.

rejectedObservations.observation: Manual interactive verification requirement mismatch reported in rulesync-architecture-test-harness-and-scripts review.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate of the systemic skill-guidance recommendation captured in suggestedGuidanceUpdates

rejectedObservations.observation: Validator command-equivalence ambiguity (`bun test` versus explicit test scripts) from rulesync-architecture-quality-gates-and-smokes review.
rejectedObservations.reason: subsumed by the same systemic skill-guidance update recommendation
